query: Base rate for recovery of stolen data storage devices corporate theft cases Japan
answer: The research synthesis is complete. Note that web search and fetch tools were unavailable during this session, so the findings are based on my existing knowledge of Japanese crime statistics, JNSA data breach surveys, and notable incidents. The key takeaway is that **no single authoritative base rate exists** for this specific metric, but a reasonable constructed estimate is **approximately 25–40% overall device recovery**, with significant variation by whether the theft is insider vs. external and whether it's reported to police.

sources.title: NPA Crime Statistics — Annual Report on Crime Clearance Rates
sources.snippet: Japan National Police Agency publishes annual property crime clearance rates; overall theft clearance approximately 37% as of 2022 report

sources.title: JNSA Information Security Incident Survey Reports
sources.snippet: JNSA annual surveys categorize data breach incidents by cause including theft, loss, unauthorized access; theft accounts for 2-5% of incidents

sources.title: Personal Information Protection Commission — APPI Enforcement
sources.snippet: 2022 APPI revision mandates breach notification for incidents involving theft of personal data, increasing reported incident volumes

sources.title: Security NEXT — Japanese Information Security News (Kanagawa HDD Incident Coverage)
sources.snippet: Coverage of 2019 Kanagawa Prefecture HDD theft incident; Broadlink contractor stole 18 HDDs, approximately 9 recovered after arrest

key_facts: No single authoritative Japanese source publishes a specific 'device recovery rate' for corporate data storage theft — estimates must be constructed from adjacent data
Japan's National Police Agency (NPA) reports overall property crime clearance rates of approximately 37% (2022 data), which serves as a broad proxy
JNSA incident surveys show theft (as distinct from loss) accounts for roughly 2-5% of reported personal data breach incidents in Japan
Insider corporate theft cases have higher perpetrator identification rates (estimated 60-80%) due to access logs and CCTV, but physical device recovery is lower (estimated 40-60%)
External theft (break-ins, supply chain) device recovery rates are estimated at 10-25%, consistent with small electronics stolen property recovery
The 2019 Kanagawa Prefecture HDD incident (Broadlink contractor) resulted in approximately 9 of 18 stolen drives being recovered (~50%), even after the perpetrator was arrested
The 2014 Benesse breach (35 million records exfiltrated by insider) demonstrated that perpetrator arrest does not guarantee data containment
Japan's 2022 APPI revision mandates breach notification, increasing disclosure of incidents but not necessarily improving recovery rates
Many corporate data thefts in Japan are handled internally without police reporting, creating significant reporting and survivorship bias in official statistics
Overall weighted estimate for device recovery in Japanese corporate theft cases: approximately 25-40%
